senses.definition: The capital city of South Africa, located in the north of Gauteng Province.
examples: She flew to Pretoria for the international conference.
Pretoria is known for its jacaranda trees that bloom in spring.

senses.definition: Relating to a president or a leader; used in some historical or ceremonial contexts to denote presidential or high-official status.
examples: The pretorial residence was decorated for the ceremony.
Pretorial duties included hosting dignitaries.

senses.definition: Historically, a member of a Roman emperor's bodyguard; in modern usage, sometimes used to refer to guards or elite troops in a ceremonial or fictional context.
examples: The guards wore pretorian-style helmets.
A character led the pretorian guard in the ceremony.

senses.definition: Plural of pretorian; members of an elite guard in historical or fictional contexts.
examples: The pretorians stood at attention.
Pretorians were celebrated in the festival narrative.

senses.definition: A Roman imperial or ceremonial building or room; in modern or fictional usage, a grand hall or landing used for official purposes.
examples: The hall served as the pretorium for state banquets.
A statue stood in the foyer of the pretorium.

senses.definition: Plural of pretor; historically used for certain magistrates or officials in ancient Rome; uncommon in modern usage.
examples: The pretors presided over civic matters.
Pretors had judicial and administrative duties.

senses.definition: The office or tenure of a pretor; the office of a magistrate or official.
examples: During his pretorship, several reform measures were proposed.
The pretorship carried significant administrative duties.

senses.definition: Plural of pretorship; terms or periods served by pretors.
examples: Several pretorships existed in the ancient city.
The pretorships were often short but influential.

senses.definition: Relating to a pretor or pretors; used to describe attributes associated with magistrates or official duties.
examples: Pretorsional duties included judicial oversight.
The pretorsional robes were ornate.

senses.definition: A hypothetical or literary term describing suffering before a torture event; not a standard common word.
examples: The story mentioned its characters' pretorture in a bleak prologue.

senses.definition: To cause or imagine suffering before torture; used only in literary or speculative contexts.
examples: The scene pretortured the protagonist to hint at future pain.

senses.definition: Having experienced or subjected to pretorture; used in literary contexts.
examples: The pretortured prisoner spoke with tremor in his voice.

senses.definition: The act of torturing or inflicting suffering before a main act of torture; used mainly in literary or speculative contexts.
examples: The guards were pretorturing prisoners in the opening scenes.

senses.definition: A preliminary or warm-up tournament before a main competition.
examples: Teams played in the pretournament to qualify.
The pretournament schedule announced before the main event.

senses.definition: Plural of pretournament; multiple warm-up tournaments.
examples: The pretournaments determined who advanced.
Several pretournaments were held across the season.

senses.definition: To trace or investigate a precursor or earlier event before a main incident; to identify an early path or cause.
examples: Investigators pretrace the origin of the malfunction.
We pretrace the signal to its source.

senses.definition: Past tense of pretrace; traced or investigated an origin or source beforehand.
examples: The path was pretraced to identify the earliest marker.

senses.definition: Relating to or located in front of the trachea; used in anatomical contexts.
examples: The pretracheal region was examined in the dissection.
Pretracheal tissue lies anterior to the trachea.

senses.definition: The act or process of tracing something previously; reviewing earlier steps or data.
examples: Pretracing the data helped identify the error.

senses.definition: Relating to cultures or practices that existed before traditional or modern traditions developed.
examples: The festival honors pretraditional customs lost to time.

senses.definition: To train someone or something in advance, often before formal or primary training.
examples: The model was pretrain on a large dataset.
We pretrain new employees with a short onboarding course.

senses.definition: Already trained in advance, typically used for models or systems.
examples: A pretrained neural network can accelerate development.
The pretrained weights improve performance.

senses.definition: The process of training a model or system in advance before fine-tuning.
examples: Pretraining helps the model learn general features.

senses.definition: Third-person singular present of pretrain; trains in advance.
examples: The system pretrains on diverse data before deployment.

senses.definition: To engage in actions or steps before a transaction occurs; used in business or technical contexts.
examples: We pretransact the agreement to ensure smooth processing.

senses.definition: An action or step taken before a transaction; often used in business or data processing contexts.
examples: Pretransaction checks help prevent errors.
